race_name: L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before January.
When someone attacks on flat paved urban and desert highway, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
70 miles on flat paved urban and desert highway burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France's flat paved urban and desert highway rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France's flat paved urban and desert highway forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 70 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France's flat paved urban and desert highway actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat paved urban and desert highway. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Base fitness for L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
At L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France’s 70 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, VO2max, and Durability to prepare you for 70 miles of flat paved urban and desert highway in Dubai Design District (D3) to Expo Village Park, Dubai, UAE.
